title: Bitbucket の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article heavily relies on Bitbucket App Passwords, which were fully deprecated and ceased to function on June 9, 2026, making the primary solutions provided obsolete as of the current date (June 15, 2026).

A critical factual error exists regarding basic authentication: while the article states to use a username with App Passwords, the current recommended API token authentication requires the Atlassian account email address as the username, which is a direct contradiction.

The default expiration time for OAuth access tokens (stated as 2 hours) appears outdated or context-specific, with more recent documentation indicating longer, configurable durations or 1-hour defaults for other contexts.
The recommended solutions are not aligned with Bitbucket's current authentication practices (API tokens are the standard replacement for App Passwords).
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty
required_actions: Perform a complete rewrite to remove all references to App Passwords as a current solution, explicitly stating their deprecation and the transition to API tokens.
Update all code examples and explanations to use Bitbucket API tokens for authentication.
Correct the guidance on basic authentication username: clarify that for API tokens, the Atlassian account email address should be used, not the Bitbucket username.
Update the information regarding OAuth access token expiration to reflect current Bitbucket Cloud policies, providing up-to-date defaults or configurable options.
Add a prominent warning or note about the deprecation of App Passwords at the beginning of the article.
Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.

sources.title: Bitbucket deprecated App Passwords - Private Packagist
sources.claim: Bitbucket announced that they deprecated app passwords in favor of their new API token system. Bitbucket app passwords will stop working entirely on June 9th, 2026.

sources.title: Deprecation of Bitbucket Cloud app passwords - Oracle Help Center
sources.claim: In 2025, Atlassian announced that they're deprecating app passwords in favor of API tokens. Existing Bitbucket Cloud app passwords will remain supported until June 9, 2026, after which app passwords will be permanently disabled.

sources.title: Enhancing security in Bitbucket: Introducing expiry for access tokens - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: When creating a new access token at the workspace, project, or repository level, users must set an expiration date that adheres to the admin-defined limit (the default maximum is 365 days, unless modified by the admin).

sources.title: Bitbucket app password or access token - AWS CodeBuild
sources.claim: For Bitbucket, your app password or access token must have the following scopes: repository:read, pullrequest:read, webhook, account. Before you begin, you must add the proper permission scopes to your Bitbucket app password or access token.

sources.title: Git Commands Return Fatal: Authentication Failed | Bitbucket Data Center
sources.claim: The user has been locked out of Bitbucket Server because of too many incorrect login attempts. Windows Credential Manager sometimes messes with stored GIT passwords causing authentication failure. Verify that the user can login to the web interface of Bitbucket Server.

sources.title: Bitbucket Authentication Failed - EDUCBA
sources.claim: There are many reasons for the authentication failing in Bitbucket... such as the Apache proxy server removing the authorization header from the Bitbucket server. Another reason is if the user has been locked due to incorrect login attempts.

sources.title: Getting 401 HTTP error when using Basic authentication with Bitbucket Cloud REST APIs
sources.claim: A 401 error indicates incorrect credentials. Up until June 9th 2026 you can use the Bitbucket username and an app password for authentication with Bitbucket Cloud APIs. Your Atlassian account email will not work when using an app password. Starting June 9th 2026 API tokens will be the only way to use Basic authentication with Bitbucket Cloud's REST APIs. In this case: Ensure that the parameter <user> in the API call is replaced with your Atlassian account email address. The Bitbucket username or the token name will NOT work when using API tokens.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud enters phase two of app password deprecation - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: Beginning September 9, 2025, Bitbucket Cloud will no longer allow the creation of new app passwords. Existing app passwords will remain valid during this phase, so your current integrations will not be interrupted. They'll continue to work until June 9, 2026. The final phase will take place on June 9, 2026, when all remaining app passwords will be permanently disabled.

sources.title: Resolve authentication error - "remote: Invalid username or password" in Bitbucket Cloud
sources.claim: If your organization manages your account or you've enabled two-step verification, create an app password to log in. The incorrect user account is logged into - to verify this please make sure you are using the Bitbucket username. Clear stored git credentials on your machine. Your account's password will not work, and app passwords will stop working after June 9th, 2026.

sources.title: Two 401 responses for every Git operation | Bitbucket Data Center
sources.claim: A possible workaround would be to use SSH to access GIT repos instead of HTTP since will reduce the 401 error entries in the logs.

sources.title: How to Add an SSH Key to Bitbucket and Clone Your Repository Securely (Step-by-Step Guide) | by Shivam Garud | Medium
sources.claim: No password prompts during Git operations. Highly secure because passwords are never transmitted. Perfect for automation, CI/CD pipelines, and multi-machine setups. SSH keys are essential for secure, efficient Git operations. Once set up: Passwords disappear. Automation becomes seamless. Multiple devices and projects remain secure.

sources.title: Using App passwords | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: App passwords are being removed. App passwords are undergoing a series of controlled brownouts starting on Jun 9, 2026 and will be permanently removed on Jul 28, 2026. All integrations using app passwords should start using API tokens with scopes immediately. We recommend connecting to Bitbucket Cloud using SSH keys when interacting with the Git command line interface. To authenticate with Bitbucket Cloud using an App password, you will need the App password and the user's Bitbucket username (not the App password label or the email address used for logging into Bitbucket and other Atlassian apps).

sources.title: Require access token expiry | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: We recommend setting a maximum expiry date range of 365 days (one year) for your team's access tokens in Bitbucket Cloud. As a workspace admin, you can set a maximum number of days tokens can be used for all access tokens created within a workspace.

sources.title: App password permissions | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: App passwords are single purpose, user-based access tokens which can be created with limited permissions (or scope). The following types of permissions are available for App passwords: Account. Workspace membership. Projects. Repositories. Pull requests. Issues. Wikis. Snippets. Webhooks. Pipelines. Runners.

sources.title: Bitbucket app password deprecation · Issue #12482 - GitHub
sources.claim: Bitbucket is deprecating app passwords and replacing them with API tokens... For API access it requires the email address and for git it's the username.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud transitions to API tokens: enhancing security with app password deprecation - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: Phase 2: Disabling new app password creation (September 9th 2025). Phase 3: Full deprecation of app passwords (June 9th 2026). App passwords will cease to function, and integrations using them will stop working.

sources.title: Bitbucket OAuth 2.0 provider API - Atlassian Documentation
sources.claim: To retrieve a new access_token , use the refresh_token parameter. Refresh tokens may be used even after the access_token itself expires.

sources.title: Getting intermittent authentication problem with BitBucket Server while doing git clone or git pull. - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Here the git-credential store is the reason for the failure. Once a password gets stored in it, it remains there and those credentials are used for the user authentication for git operations. Unset the git the credential helper using the below command before running a git pull/clone.

sources.title: Bitbucket Authentication, 2FA and OAuth | Git-Credential-Manager-for-Windows
sources.claim: Bitbucket's Access Token's expire after 1 hour if not revoked. Bitbucket implements refresh Tokens. Refresh Tokens are issued to the client application at the same time as Access Tokens. They can only be used to request a new Access Token.

sources.title: OAuth 2.0 and API authentication changes for Bitbucket Cloud
sources.claim: Unused refresh tokens will expire after 3 months, requiring full 3LO re-authorization.

sources.title: Setting up SSH Keys for Github, GitLab, and Bitbucket in one windows pc - DEV Community
sources.claim: Using SSH has several advantages over HTTPS. First, it is more secure because it uses encryption to protect your credentials. Second, it is more convenient because you don't need to enter your username and password every time you push or pull code. Finally, it allows for more fine-grained control over access to your repositories.

sources.title: Automatically generate and refresh OAuth 2.0 tokens in Bitbucket Data Center
sources.claim: The OAuth token can easily be rotated every 2h using option b on the script. Refresh tokens may be used even after the access_token itself expires.

sources.title: Using SSH keys to secure Git operations | Bitbucket Data Center 10.3
sources.claim: You can add a personal SSH key to your user account to easily authenticate when performing read operations from your local machine.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud support - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Cloud support. Documentation. Get help using and administering apps. Find troubleshooting articles. Find answers, support, and inspiration from other Atlassian users.

sources.title: Enable two-step verification | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Two-step verification secures your account by requiring a second confirmation, in addition to your password, to access Bitbucket Cloud. You will need to confirm that applications, like Git clients and automated build and deploy tools, use either SSH or an application password to access Bitbucket.

sources.title: API request failed with status 401. Check your account and app password and try again.
sources.claim: Receiving a 401 usually means your credentials are invalid or you lack permissions. Try creating a new app password with all the permissions checked and set its value in the variable BITBUCKET_APP_PASSWORD you are using in your build.

sources.title: BitBucket OAuth2.0 access for web application - HiBit
sources.claim: Access tokens expire in two hours. When this happens you'll get 401 responses.

sources.title: OAuth 2.0 provider system properties | Bitbucket Data Center 8.7 - Atlassian Documentation
sources.claim: atlassian.oauth2.provider.access.token.expiration.seconds. Default, 3600 (1 hour).

sources.title: Do I have to use app passwords? : r/bitbucket - Reddit
sources.claim: In particular if you are using multi factor authentication or some SSO solutions you would need to use an app password or ssh key, because most git clients can only user a single username and password.

sources.title: Bitbucket Account Settings
sources.claim: Official link to Bitbucket Account Settings for verifying 2FA status.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud support - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Official Bitbucket Cloud support page for reporting issues.
